what_it_does: Records a tentative recommendation for integrating Obsidian as a read-only mirror of a noticeboard ledger, proposing a staged, reversible path and noting an alternative quick-look approach.
observations: Recommends a mirror-first (A2) integration rather than opening the canonical folder directly (A1).
Proposes four stages: L0/L1 one-way sync, L2 graph shim, cross-scheme stub policy, and optional inbox writeback.
Stresses read-only access to the canonical store and avoiding in-place mutation.
Mentions concrete Obsidian affordances: search, Properties, Bases board-views, graph view, backlinks, iOS app + Sync.
Includes a contrarian 'point-straight' case for a zero-code quick demo.
References prior sections (§1 grid, §5 writeback, §7 cross-scheme stubs) without defining them.
Authored by ch-3mpkjg3r in channel dragnet-development, linked as part_of item-4baaab55.
standouts: Mirror-first architecture to make the host a reader, not a writer, by structure.
Staged spine where each stage is independently valuable and reversible.
Explicit contrarian case kept alive for a quick look.
conventions: Uses noticeboard item YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps, and history.
Markdown section heading matches the title.
Uses bold labels for each recommendation stage.
concerns: Assumes undefined prior sections (§1, §5, §7) are understood by readers.
Recommendation is 'held tentatively' and explicitly provisional.
Does not specify sync mechanism, frequency, or conflict handling beyond one-way.
Contains no code or concrete implementation details.
notes: A tentative design note arguing for a read-only Obsidian mirror as the safest integration path, with a quick-look alternative noted.
questions: What are the exact contents of §1, §5, and §7?
What sync tool or format conversion will implement L0/L1?
What does the L2 graph shim generate specifically — file names, paths, templates?
How will board:// links resolve inside Obsidian?
What is the timeline for the 'image work' referenced for image-embed stubs?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a tentative recommendation for the cleanest integration path; the file delivers exactly that recommendation plus an alternative, so it matches.
apparent_themes: Mobile/iOS as a primary use case.
intention_signals: It is trying to steer the project toward a low-risk, read-only Obsidian integration that delivers mobile value immediately while keeping the canonical ledger untouched, and to preserve optionality for a faster demo path.
novelty: The explicit staging of integration depth (L0/L1 read-only sync, L2 graph shim, stubs, writeback) and the structural argument that a one-way mirror prevents accidental promotion of Obsidian to writer.
contribution: A decision record capturing a reversible, mirror-first integration strategy and an explicit quick-look alternative, which a fused system could keep as a rationale for keeping the canonical store write-protected from Obsidian.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard recommendation item proposing a staged, read-only mirror of the ledger into Obsidian as the safest integration path, with a noted quick-look alternative.
intention_for_embedding: To recommend a tentative, low-risk Obsidian integration strategy that prioritizes mobile viewing and preserves the canonical store.
